statements.factCheckNote: FT reports with evidence of broker activity. Pentagon categorically denies. Under scrutiny. BlackRock declined to comment.
statements.source: CNBC — Hegseth defence investments Iran war

statements.text: The upcoming days will be decisive. Iran knows that, and there's nothing they can militarily do about it. We are negotiating with bombs. If Iran is wise, it will cut a deal. Trump doesn't bluff.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing with Gen. Dan Caine. Revealed secret trip to Middle East. Did not rule out ground forces.
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News, Stars and Stripes, Military Times confirmed briefing statements.

statements.text: Iran's defense industrial base is nearing complete destruction. Past 24 hours saw the lowest number of enemy missiles fired by Iran. If Iran doesn't deal, we will continue with even more intensity.
statements.context: Follow-up briefing. Visited pilots 'who deliver bombs deep into Iran.' Said talks 'gaining strength.' CENTCOM claims 75% of Iranian missile launchers destroyed.
statements.factCheckNote: war.gov, CBS News confirmed. 'Lowest missile fire' claim contradicted by IRGC firing 3 waves same day.

statements.text: The upcoming days will be decisive. American firepower is only increasing while Iran's is decreasing. We'll negotiate with bombs. This is not a so-called regime change war — but the regime sure did change.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing with Gen. Dan Caine
statements.factCheckNote: Self-contradictory on regime change. Religious rhetoric noted by NPR.

statements.text: Iran is the greatest threat to world peace. A nuclear Iran is an existential threat to the State of Israel and to all civilization.
statements.context: In an address to a joint session of Congress, Netanyahu framed the Iran nuclear issue as a global civilizational threat, seeking to build international support for action.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran's nuclear program poses legitimate proliferation concerns, characterizing it as the 'greatest threat to world peace' and 'all civilization' is considered hyperbolic by most international security analysts, who cite multiple concurrent global threats.

statements.text: Israel will not allow Iran to acquire nuclear weapons. We reserve the right to act alone if necessary.
statements.context: At the UN General Assembly, Netanyahu reiterated Israel's red line on Iranian nuclear capability, signaling willingness for unilateral military action.
statements.factCheckDetail: This accurately represents Israel's longstanding stated policy position. Israel's Begin Doctrine, established after the 1981 Osirak reactor strike, holds that Israel will prevent hostile states from acquiring nuclear weapons.

statements.text: Iran's attack on Israeli soil was an unprecedented act of aggression. We will respond at a time and manner of our choosing.
statements.context: Following Iran's direct missile and drone attack on Israel in April 2024, Netanyahu addressed the nation and pledged a calculated response.
statements.factCheckDetail: Iran did conduct a direct military strike on Israeli territory in April 2024, the first such direct attack. The characterization of the event as unprecedented and aggressive is factually supported.

statements.text: We welcome the return of President Trump. Israel has never had a greater friend in the White House.
statements.context: After the 2024 U.S. election, Netanyahu praised Trump's victory and anticipated closer coordination on Iran policy.
statements.factCheckDetail: The characterization of Trump as Israel's greatest White House friend is subjective. While Trump recognized Jerusalem and the Golan Heights, other presidents have provided significant support including military aid packages and diplomatic backing.

statements.text: Our intelligence indicates Iran is months away from a nuclear weapon. The world cannot afford to wait.
statements.context: Netanyahu presented intelligence briefings to allied leaders claiming Iran had crossed critical enrichment thresholds, building the case for preventive action.
statements.factCheckDetail: Israeli intelligence on Iran's nuclear timeline has historically been contested. Previous claims of imminent Iranian nuclear capability dating back to the 1990s did not materialize. Independent IAEA assessments noted increased enrichment but did not confirm weaponization timelines.

statements.text: Two years after October 7th, Israel is stronger and more determined than ever. We will eliminate every threat to our existence.
statements.context: On the second anniversary of the October 7 attacks, Netanyahu connected the ongoing Iran threat to the broader security environment, using the memorial to justify expanded military operations.
statements.factCheckDetail: Claims of increased strength are difficult to independently verify. While Israel's military capabilities remain formidable, the prolonged multi-front conflicts have strained resources and personnel according to IDF reserve force assessments.

statements.text: We will not live under the shadow of a nuclear threat. Tonight, Israel acts to ensure its survival for generations.
statements.context: Netanyahu's address to the nation as Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities commenced, framing the operation as an existential necessity.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the nuclear threat concern has some basis, the characterization of the strikes as purely defensive is contested. Critics argue Israel possesses its own undeclared nuclear arsenal, creating a deterrence dynamic that the 'survival' framing does not acknowledge.

statements.text: Our operations have been precise and limited to military and nuclear targets. We have no quarrel with the Iranian people.
statements.context: Amid reports of civilian casualties near targeted facilities, Netanyahu defended the operation's targeting methodology and attempted to separate the conflict from the Iranian civilian population.
statements.factCheckDetail: While strikes may have targeted military and nuclear facilities, independent reports from humanitarian organizations documented civilian casualties in surrounding areas. Infrastructure damage also affected civilian populations through power and water disruptions.

statements.text: We are open to a ceasefire if Iran permanently dismantles its nuclear program and its proxy network. These are reasonable terms.
statements.context: Under growing international pressure, Netanyahu set conditions for a ceasefire that were widely seen as non-starters designed to appear diplomatic while ensuring continued operations.
statements.factCheckDetail: Demanding permanent dismantlement of both nuclear programs and proxy networks as preconditions for ceasefire is considered diplomatically unrealistic. Analysts note these conditions exceed what was achieved through years of JCPOA negotiations and are designed to be unacceptable.

statements.text: Israel will continue wide-scale strikes on Iran's military and energy infrastructure until the threat is eliminated.
statements.context: IDF briefing.

statements.text: We are breaking their bones. The moment of truth is close. We are trying to free Iran. There will be many surprises in the next phase.
statements.context: Netanyahu addressed the Iranian people directly, vowing 'many surprises.' Diverging from Trump's 'war ending soon' signals — Netanyahu wants to continue operations while Trump pushes de-escalation. Bloomberg reports widening gap between US and Israeli endgames.
statements.factCheckDetail: Netanyahu frames strikes as liberation of Iranians, but 1,380+ civilians killed undermines this framing. IDF has struck 170+ munitions in Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz including IRGC Quds Force HQ.

statements.text: If Lebanon does not rein in Hezbollah, we will strike national infrastructure. We have the capability and the will to take territory.
statements.context: Threat conveyed through US and Western channels to Lebanon's government. Defense Minister Katz made similar statements. Follows largest Hezbollah barrage of the war.

statements.text: We are crushing Iran and Hezbollah. Mojtaba Khamenei is an IRGC puppet who cannot show his face in public. To the Iranian people: the coming days will bring an opportunity to free yourselves. Be ready.
statements.context: First press conference since war began. Netanyahu vowed 'many surprises' for the next phase.
statements.factCheckNote: Broadcast live; quotes confirmed by Times of Israel, PBS, Haaretz

statements.text: Israel's defense chief has declared we will hold Lebanese territory until Hezbollah fully disarms. There will be no withdrawal without security guarantees.
statements.context: Day 14 statement via defense establishment channels. Israel defense chief announced intention to hold occupied Lebanese land until Hezbollah disarms — echoing 2006 war posture but with expanded territorial aims.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz and CNN reporting on Israel defense chief statements.

statements.text: This IRGC puppet Mojtaba Khamenei can't even show his face in public. This is not an endless war — it will usher in an era of peace. There will be many surprises for the next phase.
statements.context: First press conference of the war and Fox News Hannity interview. Scoffed at new Supreme Leader's inability to appear publicly.
statements.factCheckNote: Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement was read by state TV anchor, not delivered in person.

statements.text: Our strikes are creating optimal conditions for toppling the regime, but I cannot guarantee regime change without an internal uprising. I wouldn't take out a life insurance policy on any of their leaders.
statements.context: Press conference addressing regime change goals and threats against Iranian leadership
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Bloomberg; press conference footage available

statements.text: [Video posted showing himself alive and well]
statements.context: Netanyahu posted a video to counter Iranian state media rumors that he had been killed. The video showed him in an undisclosed location, appearing unharmed.
statements.factCheckNote: Video confirmed authentic. Posted in direct response to Iranian propaganda claiming Netanyahu was dead.

statements.text: I am dying... for a coffee.
statements.context: Netanyahu posted video from Tel Aviv cafe mocking conspiracy theories about his death. Spread all five fingers visibly to counter AI deepfake '6 finger' claims.
statements.factCheckNote: Reuters confirmed Netanyahu's presence via cafe surveillance cameras. Snopes debunked the 6-finger deepfake claim.

statements.text: We will eradicate Hezbollah from Lebanon. The IDF's ground operation will continue until the threat to our northern border is eliminated.
statements.context: Statement following IDF ground operations into southern Lebanon. 91st Division took Khiam. 450,000 reservists called up.
statements.factCheckNote: IDF confirmed ground operations in southern Lebanon. However, 'eradicate' language is aspirational given Hezbollah's deep roots in Lebanese society.

statements.text: Go out and celebrate. We're watching from above.
statements.context: Video message urging Iranians to celebrate Chaharshanbe Suri fire festival as anti-regime protest
statements.factCheckNote: Video confirmed by PM's office

statements.text: We are watching you from above. The brave people of Iran deserve a year of freedom.
statements.context: Nowruz greeting video from Kirya HQ, referencing Larijani/Soleimani kills
statements.factCheckNote: JPost confirmed

statements.text: The strike on South Pars sends a clear message: there is no safe haven for Iran's military infrastructure. We will continue striking until Iran's ability to threaten Israel is fully degraded.
statements.context: Statement after IAF strikes on South Pars gas field and Bandar Anzali naval base
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Times of Israel

statements.text: Iran can no longer enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles. Israel needs a ground component for regime change. I see this war ending a lot faster than people think. Iran is no longer the same Iran.
statements.context: Evening press conference, March 19. First explicit call for ground offensive and regime change.
statements.factCheckNote: No evidence provided for uranium enrichment claim. IAEA chief Grossi: 'the material will still be there.' Ground offensive claim represents major policy shift.

statements.text: Iran can no longer enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles. Regime change requires 'ground component.' On South Pars: 'Israel acted alone. President Trump asked us to hold off, and we're holding off.'
statements.context: 2nd press conference since war
statements.factCheckNote: Nuclear/missile claims unverified independently

statements.text: Mocked Mojtaba: 'I am not sure who is ruling Iran. Mojtaba, the replacement Ayatollah, has yet to appear.' Denied dragging US into war.
statements.context: Press conference
statements.factCheckNote: Broadcast live

statements.text: Confirmed Israel struck Tehran again on Nowruz/Persian New Year. On IRGC spokesman Naeini's claim Iran was still building missiles: 'He was wrong. Dead wrong.' Netanyahu stated Israel will hold off future attacks on energy sites at Trump's request: 'President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding off.'
statements.context: Statement on Day 21 strikes; Naeini killed shortly after his claim Iran still building missiles
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog March 20 confirmed; Fox News and NPR confirmed.

statements.text: President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding out. Israel acted alone in hitting the South Pars gas field.
statements.context: Statement on Nowruz; confirming Trump-Netanyahu rift on energy infrastructure targeting
statements.factCheckNote: Directly confirmed by Netanyahu; consistent with Trump statement

statements.text: Fact number one, Israel acted alone against the Asaluyeh gas compound. Fact number two, President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding off.
statements.context: First English-language press conference since the war began. Netanyahu admitted Israel unilaterally struck South Pars (Asaluyeh), contradicting earlier reports that the US had advance knowledge. Confirmed agreement with Trump to pause energy infrastructure strikes unless Iran attacks Qatar.
statements.factCheckNote: Netanyahu's claim that Israel 'acted alone' contradicts reporting by anonymous Israeli officials who initially said the US had advance knowledge of the South Pars strike.

statements.text: You can't make a revolution from the air... there must be a ground component — I won't share with you all the options.
statements.context: At the same press conference, signaling that Israeli planning includes a ground component, raising concerns about a possible land invasion of Iran or Lebanon escalation.
statements.factCheckNote: Forward-looking strategic statement; not a factual claim.

statements.text: This is a very difficult evening in the campaign for our future. We are determined to continue to strike our enemies on all fronts.
statements.context: Statement issued after Iranian ballistic missiles struck Arad (74 injured, air defense failure) and Dimona (47 injured, adjacent to nuclear research center) on Day 22 evening.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by France 24, CBS News live updates, multiple Israeli media.

statements.text: If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have proved it. They fired on Jerusalem right next to the holy sites of the three monotheistic faiths. They have the capacity to reach deep into Europe. It's time to see the leaders of the rest of the countries join up.
statements.context: Statement at Arad — site of the Day 22 Iranian IRBM direct hit — urging Europe to join military coalition against Iran. Cited Iran's Diego Garcia strike as proof Berlin, Paris, and Rome are within reach.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's Diego Garcia IRBM strike (intercepted, ~4,000km) confirmed by WSJ/CNBC. The ~4,000km range is factually within range of European capitals.

statements.text: If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have given it. It's time for world leaders to join us, and I'm seeing movement from some in that direction. What more proof do you need that this regime that threatens the entire world has to be stopped?
statements.context: Netanyahu spoke at the missile strike site in Arad, where Iranian ballistic missiles struck residential buildings overnight wounding 115+ people. He cited three events: Arad attack, missiles fired toward Jerusalem near holy sites, and Diego Garcia ICBM attempt.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel. Netanyahu visited Arad on March 22.

statements.text: President Trump's call to have the international community confront this fanatic terrorist regime of zealots is a call not only for the security of America and the security of Israel; it's for the security of the entire world. It's time for them to act.
statements.context: Statement at Arad missile strike site, elaborating on coalition-building appeal after Trump's call for global participation in the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Tribune India/ANI and Jerusalem Post reporting from Arad visit.

statements.text: Whatever we do, we do together, and as far as possible, in confidence. President Trump sees an opportunity to achieve the war's objectives through an agreement with Iran. Israel will be very happy with the terms Trump has described. Our attacks on Iran and Lebanon continue regardless of diplomatic signals.
statements.context: Netanyahu backed US strikes on Iran's power grid if Tehran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and supported Trump's claim of productive talks while vowing Israeli operations continue at full force.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera video newsfeed and Haaretz live updates. IDF simultaneously confirmed operations continue 'at full force.'
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Netanyahu backs US strikes on Iran

statements.text: Israel will protect our vital interests in every situation. Iran's attack on Diego Garcia proves they have the capacity to reach deep into Europe. They are putting everyone in their sights — it is time for the leaders of the rest of the countries to join up.
statements.context: Netanyahu first remarks since Trump announced Iran talks. Called on world leaders to join coalition, warning Europe of Iranian IRBM range revealed by Diego Garcia attack (March 21). Separately spoke with VP JD Vance about deal components.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran confirmed the Diego Garcia attack. Missile range 4,000km verified by UK government. Call with Vance confirmed by Israeli officials.

statements.text: I believe it is possible to leverage the mighty achievements obtained by the US and Israeli military to realize the goals of the war in an agreement that will safeguard our vital interests. Israel acted alone in striking the Asaluyeh gas complex. We are complying with President Trump's request to pause further such strikes.
statements.context: Statement after Trump's announcement of US-Iran talks. Netanyahu confirmed Israel was acting in coordination with Trump, and confirmed Israel alone struck South Pars/Asaluyeh gas compound.
statements.factCheckNote: Israeli strike on South Pars/Asaluyeh independently confirmed. Compliance with Trump request confirmed by multiple Israeli officials.

statements.text: We are continuing to strike in both Iran and Lebanon. We are smashing the missile program and the nuclear program, and we continue to deal severe blows to Hezbollah… there is more to come. I believe it is possible to leverage the mighty achievements obtained by the US and Israeli military to realize the goals of the war in an agreement.
statements.context: Netanyahu video statement on Day 25, reaffirming the war will continue. Despite Trump's 5-day pause on energy infrastructure strikes, Israel continued striking Iran. Netanyahu is also reportedly frustrated that Mossad's promised Iranian popular uprising has not materialized.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 25 and Haaretz. Nuclear program strikes confirmed by IDF.

statements.text: The IDF needs a few more weeks to fully degrade Iranian military capabilities. Israel will not stop until its security objectives are achieved. Targeted assassinations of Iranian leaders and commanders will continue.
statements.context: Attributed to senior Israeli security official briefing; Netanyahu instructed IDF to maximize strikes in 48-hour window before possible Trump deal
statements.factCheckNote: Statement attributed to senior Israeli official per ABC News. Netanyahu's rush to maximize strikes before potential ceasefire reported by CNN and Times of Israel, citing multiple current/former officials.

statements.text: Iran is being decimated. We are winning. But to truly achieve our objectives, there must be a ground component alongside the bombing. You don't want to replace one ayatollah with another. We are standing by the Iranian people's side.
statements.context: Netanyahu pushing the Trump administration for a ground invasion component to achieve regime change, amid concern that Trump may end the war before Israel's objectives are met
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC, Jerusalem Post, and Times of Israel reporting. Statement reflects ongoing US-Israel tension over war objectives.
statements.source: CNBC — Netanyahu: Trump, US-Israel need ground component alongside Iran bombing

statements.text: It is often said that you can't win — you can't do revolutions from the air... but there has to be a ground component, as well. The countries of Europe: they have the capacity to reach deep into Europe. If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have proved it.
statements.context: Netanyahu pushing for a ground component to operations in Iran, warning European leaders that Iran's missile range extends to Europe and citing the last 48 hours as proof.
statements.venue: Security cabinet session / Statement to European leaders
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill and Jerusalem Post. Netanyahu previously denied any ground operation plans publicly.

statements.text: We have achieved more than half our war aims. We are close to finishing Iran's arms industry. I don't want to put a schedule on it. Thousands of IRGC members have been killed.
statements.context: Newsmax interview, claiming war has passed its midpoint. Contradicted by RUSI report that Israel/US running low on interceptors.
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of 'beyond halfway' contradicted by RUSI report on interceptor shortages and Haaretz reporting Iran still able to hit Haifa refinery.

statements.text: I won't put a timeline on when the war will end, but over half our missions have been achieved.
statements.context: Statement amid Knesset approval of record $45.8B defense budget
statements.factCheckNote: 'Over half missions achieved' claim cannot be independently verified. IDF operational details classified.

statements.text: The war has achieved more than half its aims. We are definitely beyond the halfway point. Israel and the US are close to finishing Iran's arms industry.
statements.context: Hebrew-language video statement on Day 32
statements.factCheckNote: No public metric for 'halfway point' provided. Claim about arms industry contradicted by continued IRGC missile launches.

statements.text: We had to act, and we acted. We have changed the face of the Middle East. We will continue to crush the terror regime in Tehran.
statements.context: Passover-eve televised statement. Told Newsmax war progress 'beyond the halfway point.' Hours later, IRGC fired 3 missile waves at Israel.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Euronews confirmed. 'Changed the face of Middle East' claim contradicted by ongoing Iranian missile attacks same day.

statements.text: We have inflicted ten plagues on Iran's axis of evil. Iran's trillion-dollar investment in terrorism has gone down the drain. Our campaign, together with the United States, is not yet over.
statements.context: Passover Eve address drawing Biblical parallels to the war
statements.factCheckNote: Nuclear capability destruction claims disputed by IAEA and arms control experts.

statements.text: The Zionist regime is a cancerous tumor that the resistance front will ultimately uproot. This is a divine promise.
statements.context: In an address marking the anniversary of the Islamic Revolution, Khamenei used extreme rhetoric about Israel while framing Iranian regional strategy in religious terms.
statements.factCheckDetail: This is ideological and religious rhetoric rather than a factual claim. It represents the Supreme Leader's longstanding stated position on Israel, though the practical implications of such statements are debated among analysts.

statements.text: America's maximum pressure failed before and it will fail again. Iran's economy has proven resilient against sanctions.
statements.context: Responding to renewed U.S. sanctions rhetoric, Khamenei pointed to Iran's adaptation to previous sanctions as evidence of resilience.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran's economy did not collapse under sanctions, it suffered significant contraction, currency devaluation, and reduced oil exports. 'Resilient' masks genuine economic hardship experienced by ordinary Iranians, including inflation exceeding 40%.

statements.text: Our response to Israeli aggression demonstrated that Iran will defend itself. The era of unanswered attacks is over.
statements.context: After Iran's direct missile and drone attack on Israel, Khamenei framed the operation as a new deterrence paradigm, claiming Iran had established a precedent of direct retaliation.
statements.factCheckDetail: Iran's April 2024 attack on Israel did represent a significant escalation and departure from Iran's previous policy of operating primarily through proxies. The characterization of establishing a new norm of direct retaliation is factually supported by the event.

statements.text: Trump's return changes nothing. Iran does not negotiate under threats. Our nuclear program is peaceful and non-negotiable.
statements.context: On the day of Trump's inauguration, Khamenei issued a statement rejecting any preconditions for negotiations and reaffirming Iran's stated position on its nuclear program.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim that Iran's nuclear program is entirely peaceful is contradicted by IAEA reports documenting enrichment to 60% purity, far beyond civilian energy needs. Iran has also restricted IAEA inspector access, undermining transparency claims.

statements.text: We possess the technical capability to defend our nation by any means necessary. Our scientists' achievements cannot be bombed away.
statements.context: In a statement widely interpreted as a veiled reference to nuclear weapons capability, Khamenei used ambiguous language that stopped short of declaring a weapons program while implying deterrent capacity.
statements.factCheckDetail: The deliberately ambiguous language makes fact-checking difficult. Intelligence agencies disagree on whether Iran has achieved weapons-breakout capability. The claim that knowledge cannot be destroyed by bombing is generally true regarding scientific expertise, though infrastructure can be damaged.

statements.text: The Zionist enemy has made a grave miscalculation. They have opened the gates of hell upon themselves.
statements.context: Hours after Israeli strikes began on Iranian nuclear facilities, Khamenei issued a brief statement promising severe retaliation.
statements.factCheckDetail: This is a threat and rhetorical statement rather than a verifiable factual claim. The characterization of the strikes as a 'miscalculation' represents Iran's strategic assessment but cannot be independently verified.

statements.text: The resistance axis stands united. Every attack on Iran strengthens our allies' resolve from Beirut to Baghdad to Sana'a.
statements.context: As Iranian-aligned groups across the region launched retaliatory attacks, Khamenei framed the conflict as a validation of Iran's alliance network.
statements.venue: Address to Supreme National Security Council
statements.factCheckDetail: While proxy groups did escalate attacks, reports indicated coordination challenges and some groups acting independently. Hezbollah's capacity had been significantly degraded by prior Israeli operations in Lebanon, undermining the claim of unified strength.

statements.text: We will never surrender. Iran has endured for thousands of years and will outlast this aggression as it has outlasted all others.
statements.context: In a national address as the conflict intensified, Khamenei invoked Persian historical resilience to rally domestic support amid reports of significant infrastructure damage.
statements.factCheckDetail: The historical framing is largely rhetorical. Iran does have a long civilizational history, but the analogy to current geopolitical conflict is more motivational than analytical. The practical outcome of the current conflict remains undetermined.

statements.text: I believe in constructive engagement with the world. Iran's security is best served through diplomacy, not isolation.
statements.context: During his presidential campaign, Pezeshkian distinguished himself from hardline candidates by advocating for renewed diplomatic engagement with the West.
statements.factCheckDetail: This accurately represents Pezeshkian's stated campaign platform, which emphasized diplomatic engagement. His election was widely seen as a public mandate for moderation, though the Supreme Leader retains final authority on foreign policy.

statements.text: Iran is ready to return to the negotiating table without preconditions. We seek a deal that respects our sovereignty and rights.
statements.context: In his debut address at the UN General Assembly as president, Pezeshkian extended an olive branch to Western nations, signaling openness to new nuclear negotiations.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Pezeshkian expressed willingness to negotiate, the Supreme Leader's office subsequently clarified that certain conditions remained, including sanctions relief before substantive talks. The president's authority on nuclear policy is limited compared to the Supreme Leader.

statements.text: The Trump administration has not responded to our diplomatic signals. We cannot negotiate with people who only speak in threats.
statements.context: After months of unanswered diplomatic overtures, Pezeshkian expressed frustration with the lack of U.S. engagement, marking a shift in his initially optimistic tone.
statements.factCheckDetail: Multiple diplomatic sources confirmed that Iranian back-channel communications were not reciprocated by the Trump administration. European mediators corroborated the lack of U.S. engagement with Iranian diplomatic proposals.

statements.text: Our nuclear program serves our energy needs and our scientific advancement. We have no interest in weapons of destruction.
statements.context: Responding to Israeli intelligence claims about Iran's nuclear progress, Pezeshkian maintained the official position that the program was entirely civilian.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran claims its nuclear program is peaceful, enrichment to 60% purity significantly exceeds civilian energy requirements (3-5%). IAEA reports have documented activities inconsistent with a purely civilian program, including restricted inspector access to certain facilities.

statements.text: I urge the international community to intervene before it is too late. There are those who want war, and we are not among them.
statements.context: As tensions escalated to critical levels, Pezeshkian made a direct appeal to the UN and European leaders, positioning Iran as seeking peace while Israel and the U.S. pushed toward conflict.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Pezeshkian's stated desire for peace may be genuine, Iran's continued enrichment activities, expansion of proxy operations, and the Supreme Leader's inflammatory rhetoric complicated the credibility of positioning Iran as solely peace-seeking.

statements.text: This unprovoked attack on our sovereign territory is an act of war. We call on all nations to condemn this aggression.
statements.context: Pezeshkian issued a formal statement condemning Israeli strikes, invoking international law and calling for a UN Security Council emergency session.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the strikes did target Iranian sovereign territory, the characterization of 'unprovoked' is contested. Israel cited Iran's nuclear advancement and support for proxy attacks as provocation. The legal classification of the strikes under international law is a matter of ongoing debate.

statements.text: Even now, I call for an immediate ceasefire and international mediation. War serves no one's interests, least of all the people.
statements.context: Breaking from the Supreme Leader's more bellicose rhetoric, Pezeshkian called for ceasefire, highlighting a visible divide between the elected presidency and the Supreme Leader's office.
statements.factCheckDetail: The call for ceasefire is a genuine statement of policy position. The divergence from the Supreme Leader's rhetoric was confirmed by multiple analysts as reflecting a real internal policy disagreement within the Iranian government.

statements.text: Millions of Iranians are displaced and suffering. The humanitarian cost of this conflict demands an immediate end to hostilities.
statements.context: Pezeshkian cited humanitarian concerns as the basis for renewed ceasefire calls, publishing casualty and displacement figures that could not be independently verified.
statements.factCheckDetail: The specific displacement figures cited by Pezeshkian could not be independently verified due to restricted access for international humanitarian organizations. That significant civilian displacement has occurred is widely accepted, but precise numbers remain contested.

statements.text: Any ceasefire must include firm international guarantees that Iran will not be attacked again. We also demand compensation for the assault on our nation and recognition of Iran's right to a full nuclear fuel cycle.
statements.context: Day 12. Pezeshkian publicly laid out Iran's conditions for ending the war, marking the first formal ceasefire demands. Deputy FM Gharibabadi confirmed back-channel communications with Russia, China, and France. Bloomberg reported the demands are unlikely to be accepted by Washington or Jerusalem.
statements.factCheckDetail: Pezeshkian's conditions confirmed by Bloomberg via officials familiar with the matter; Deputy FM Gharibabadi's statements reported by multiple outlets independently

statements.text: Iran demands three conditions for peace: recognition of our sovereign rights, reparations for the destruction caused by US-Israeli aggression, and binding guarantees against future attacks.
statements.context: First formal articulation of Iran's demands for ending hostilities. Signals Iran open to talks but with high price. Comes as Iran launches largest coordinated attack with Hezbollah.

statements.text: Set terms for ending the war: recognition of Iran's rights, reparations for damage caused, and guarantees against future aggression by the US and Israel.
statements.context: Statement on ceasefire conditions
statements.factCheckNote: Terms reported by Al Jazeera and France 24.

statements.text: Iran has never pursued nuclear arms. We propose the establishment of an Islamic Parliament of the Middle East as a regional security structure.
statements.context: Nowruz message from Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian. Called for regional unity and proposed a new 'Islamic Parliament of the Middle East.' The statement came amid reports that Pezeshkian admitted in closed conversations that he is 'completely excluded from military and strategic decisions.'
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's claim to have never pursued nuclear weapons contradicts longstanding IAEA concerns, enrichment to 60%+ levels, and Netanyahu's claims about nuclear program destruction. However, the specific nuclear fatwa Pezeshkian cites is documented.

statements.text: We do not need the foreigners' presence in the region. We can set up an Islamic assembly in the Middle East with the cooperation of Muslim states, and within that framework, regulate our security, economic, cultural and political relations.
statements.context: Nowruz (Persian New Year) address proposing a new regional security architecture. Pezeshkian called for an "Islamic Assembly of the Middle East" as a framework to replace foreign military presence. He rejected Israel's narrative and called Israel the real source of instability. Also reiterated Iran's rejection of nuclear weapons based on the Supreme Leader's religious fatwa.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ANI and Tribune India citing Iranian state media broadcast.

statements.text: There needs to be an immediate cessation of US-Israeli aggression. We call on BRICS to play an independent role. We can set up an Islamic Assembly in the Middle East with Muslim states to regulate security, economic, and political relations.
statements.context: Phone call with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Nowruz (Persian New Year). Pezeshkian proposed a regional security framework and called on BRICS nations to act independently of Western pressure.
statements.venue: Phone call with PM Narendra Modi of India
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al-Monitor and Madhyamam Online reporting on the Modi-Pezeshkian call.

statements.text: You are our brothers, and we have no dispute with you. We seek to live in peace with our neighbors.
statements.context: Eid al-Fitr message to Islamic and neighboring countries on Day 23. Signals Iran is seeking to prevent the conflict from expanding regionally while continuing to fight US and Israel.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Capital News Point and regional media.

statements.text: The illusion of erasing Iran from the map shows desperation against the will of a history-making nation. Threats and terror only strengthen our unity. The Strait of Hormuz is open to all except those who violate our soil. We firmly confront delirious threats on the battlefield.
statements.context: Posted on X in response to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum threatening to obliterate Iranian power plants if Hormuz is not opened. Also called on BRICS to 'play an independent role in halting aggressions against Iran.'
statements.factCheckNote: Post confirmed by Tribune India and Al Jazeera Day 23 explainer.

statements.text: The statements of the US president are made in the context of attempts to reduce energy prices and buy time to implement their military plans. There have been no negotiations with Washington.
statements.context: Pezeshkian Telegram post directly responding to Trump's claim of productive US-Iran talks. Called Trump's announcement an attempt to manipulate oil markets.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran denies all contact with US. However, Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt are known intermediaries — whether formal contact occurred through these channels remains disputed.

statements.text: Iran did not begin this atrocious war. Any end to the fighting is meaningless until we ensure there will be no more attacks, and that our legitimate rights are restored. We insist on war reparations and firm international guarantees. We will not accept a ceasefire that leaves Iran humiliated and unprotected.
statements.context: Statement to French President Macron during a phone call. Pezeshkian also apologized to neighboring Gulf states for damage caused by IRGC attacks — though the IRGC continued attacks independently, exposing a significant civilian-military leadership rift.
statements.venue: Phone call with French President Macron
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera and Middle East Monitor. IRGC-presidential rift analysis confirmed by multiple regional analysts.
statements.source: Middle East Monitor — Pezeshkian sets terms for ceasefire talks

statements.text: Iran seeks a complete end to the war and wants to ensure security and peace in West Asia. We consider the US-Israeli actions against our country unlawful and inhumane.
statements.context: Phone call with Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim. Pezeshkian praised Malaysia's "principled stance" condemning the attacks. Iran simultaneously announced Malaysia's ships are approved for Hormuz transit — following Spain earlier in the day. Iran allowing ships from non-hostile nations while blocking US/Israeli-linked vessels.
statements.venue: Phone call with Malaysian PM Anwar Ibrahim
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Brunei News Net and Al Jazeera reporting on Malaysia-Iran relations during the war.

statements.text: Talking to leaders of Russia and Pakistan, I reaffirmed Iran's commitment to peace in the region. The only way to end this war — ignited by the Zionist regime and the United States — is recognizing Iran's legitimate rights, payment of reparations, and firm international guarantees against future aggression.
statements.context: X post following calls with Russian and Pakistani leaders, ahead of Islamabad peace summit
statements.factCheckNote: Pezeshkian's X post confirmed. Calls with Russia and Pakistan PM confirmed by both sides.
statements.source: Pezeshkian on X via Pakistan Today

statements.text: Iran needs trust and credible guarantees before any talks can succeed. Pakistan's mediation is valuable — but the window for diplomacy requires all parties to act in good faith. Iran has laid out its conditions: recognition of legitimate rights, payment of reparations, and firm guarantees against future aggression.
statements.context: Phone call with Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif on Day 29, discussing Islamabad peace summit and Iran's conditions for ceasefire negotiations
statements.venue: Phone call with Pakistani PM Sharif
statements.factCheckNote: Call confirmed by Iranian state media and Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: Iran does not seek nuclear weapons. We have participated in a one-hour call with Prime Minister Sharif. We are open to discussions that respect Iranian sovereignty and result in a permanent end to all attacks. Peace is possible if the conditions are right.
statements.context: Phone call with Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif during Islamabad peace summit preparations; Pezeshkian reportedly signaling Iran could consider ending the war if certain conditions are met, in contrast to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's hardline stance
statements.venue: Phone call with Pakistan PM / Iranian state media
statements.factCheckNote: Phone call confirmed by Pakistan PM Sharif and Iranian state media. Pezeshkian signals mark a divergence from the Supreme Leader position on negotiations per CNN analysis.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran war live Day 30

statements.text: Iran's economy could collapse within 3-4 weeks without a ceasefire. Civilian government authority must be restored.
statements.context: Privately warned IRGC Chief Ahmad Vahidi of economic collapse. IRGC rejected demand. Inflation at ~120%, ATMs empty, internet at 4%.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Iran International citing unnamed sources. Cannot be independently confirmed due to secrecy of internal meetings.

statements.text: Iran has the necessary will to end the war if essential conditions are met, especially guarantees required to prevent repetition of aggression. European intervention would have dangerous consequences.
statements.context: Phone call with European Council President Antonio Costa. Also warned economy could collapse in 3 weeks to 1 month without ceasefire.
statements.venue: Phone call with EU Council President Costa
statements.factCheckNote: Euronews and CNN confirmed. Separately, Iran International reported serious rift with IRGC chief Vahidi.

statements.text: The Iranian people harbor no enmity toward other nations, including the people of America, Europe or neighboring countries. Which interests are being served by this war? Not America First.
statements.context: Open letter to the American people published via Bloomberg and Dawn. Strongest ceasefire signal yet from Tehran — rejected unconditional surrender, called war 'not serving US interests,' framed Iran's actions as 'legitimate self-defense.'
statements.factCheckNote: Full text published by Bloomberg, Dawn. Confirmed by Al Jazeera, US News.

statements.text: The erosion of the nuclear nonproliferation framework is deeply alarming. The JCPOA remains the best available path to preventing a nuclear crisis.
statements.context: In his annual report on disarmament, Guterres warned about the consequences of the JCPOA's collapse and urged renewed diplomatic engagement.
statements.factCheckDetail: The assessment that the JCPOA framework was eroding is supported by IAEA reports documenting reduced monitoring access and increased Iranian enrichment. The characterization of the JCPOA as the best available path reflects the consensus view of the nonproliferation community.

statements.text: I condemn the Iranian attack on Israel and call on all parties to step back from the brink. The Middle East cannot afford another war.
statements.context: Guterres responded to Iran's direct attack on Israel with a call for de-escalation, attempting to maintain the UN's position of neutrality while condemning the specific act of aggression.
statements.venue: UN Headquarters Press Statement, New York
statements.factCheckDetail: The condemnation and the characterization of the regional security situation are consistent with documented events. The call for restraint represents the official UN position.

statements.text: The failure of diplomacy is not inevitable. It is a choice made by leaders who prefer confrontation to compromise.
statements.context: Guterres expressed growing frustration with the lack of diplomatic engagement between the U.S., Israel, and Iran, placing responsibility on political leaders.
statements.venue: Address to UN General Assembly Special Session
statements.factCheckDetail: This is a normative statement reflecting the Secretary-General's assessment of diplomatic dynamics. While the characterization of diplomatic failure as a political choice is shared by many analysts, others argue structural factors beyond individual leaders' choices contributed to the breakdown.

statements.text: I am deeply concerned by reports of near-weapons-grade enrichment. I urge Iran to cooperate fully with IAEA inspectors.
statements.context: Responding to an IAEA report indicating enrichment levels approaching weapons-grade, Guterres issued a pointed statement calling for transparency.
statements.factCheckDetail: The IAEA did report enrichment levels nearing weapons-grade. Guterres's call for cooperation with inspectors reflected the official UN position and was supported by the IAEA Board of Governors' own resolutions.

statements.text: Military strikes on nuclear facilities risk catastrophic environmental and humanitarian consequences. I call for an immediate cessation of hostilities.
statements.context: Within hours of the first Israeli strikes, Guterres issued an urgent statement warning about the potential for radiological contamination and calling for a ceasefire.
statements.venue: Emergency UN Press Conference, New York
statements.factCheckDetail: The warning about environmental and humanitarian consequences of striking nuclear facilities is supported by scientific assessments from the IAEA and independent nuclear safety experts. Radiological contamination from damaged enrichment facilities is a documented risk.

statements.text: The Security Council's inability to act is a failure of the international system. Civilians are dying while permanent members exercise vetoes.
statements.context: After the U.S. vetoed a ceasefire resolution, Guterres took the unusual step of publicly criticizing the Security Council, reflecting deep frustration with institutional paralysis.
statements.venue: UN General Assembly Emergency Special Session
statements.factCheckDetail: The U.S. did veto a ceasefire resolution. Guterres's characterization of institutional failure reflects factual events. The criticism of the veto mechanism, while politically charged, is grounded in the documented inability of the Council to pass a binding resolution.

statements.text: International humanitarian law applies to all parties in this conflict. Targeting civilian infrastructure is a violation regardless of the justification offered.
statements.context: Guterres responded to reports of strikes hitting power plants, water treatment facilities, and transportation infrastructure in both Iran and Israel.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement accurately reflects international humanitarian law, specifically the Fourth Geneva Convention and Additional Protocol I, which prohibit attacks on civilian infrastructure. The legal principles cited are well-established in international law.

statements.text: I am appointing a Special Envoy and proposing a 72-hour humanitarian pause. The suffering must stop. We need space for diplomacy.
statements.context: Guterres announced concrete UN-led de-escalation measures, including the appointment of a Special Envoy and a proposed humanitarian corridor agreement.
statements.factCheckDetail: The appointment of a Special Envoy and the proposal for a humanitarian pause are concrete actions within the Secretary-General's authority. Whether parties will accept the proposal remains to be seen.

statements.text: Arrived in Beirut for solidarity visit. The conflict has caused 'immense suffering.' 'De-escalation and dialogue are the only way out.' 773 killed in Lebanon, 800,000+ displaced.
statements.context: Arrival statement in Beirut
statements.factCheckNote: Visit confirmed by UN News. Casualty figures align with known data.

statements.text: Directly addressed the warring parties from Brussels: 'To the United States and to Israel: it's high time to end this war that is risking getting completely out of control.' Also told Iran: 'Stop attacking your neighbors. They were never parties to the conflict.' Warned the Hormuz closure 'causes enormous pain in so many people around the world that have nothing to do with this conflict.'
statements.context: Briefing European Council leaders in Brussels; strongest UN statement since war began
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera video confirmed; UN News transcript confirmed.

statements.text: Stop attacking your neighbours; they were never parties to the conflict. The world cannot afford a war that spirals completely out of control. The Strait of Hormuz must be opened immediately. The United Nations stands ready to help facilitate safe passage.
statements.context: Guterres addressed the UN Security Council on March 21, calling for Iran to stop attacks on Gulf states and for the US/Israel to halt strikes before the conflict spirals further.
statements.factCheckNote: UN official statement. Confirmed by UN News and UN Press.

statements.text: To the United States and to Israel: it's high time to end this war that is risking getting completely out of control. The prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz causes enormous pain in so many people around the world that have nothing to do with this conflict. It's time for the force of the law to prevail over the law of force.
statements.context: Guterres made the remarks outside the European Council meeting in Brussels, calling for an immediate end to the conflict and warning of global economic consequences from the Hormuz closure.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by UN News and Arab News.

statements.text: This war is out of control. This has gone too far. The world is staring down the barrel of a wider war. A direct US-Iran meeting could be the last opportunity to avoid a fully fledged escalation. I am appointing veteran French diplomat Jean Arnault as my personal envoy for the Middle East conflict.
statements.context: Guterres made his strongest statement yet on the conflict, declaring it 'out of control' and announcing the appointment of Jean Arnault as personal envoy. He called on US/Israel to end the war and Iran to stop attacking neighbors. This comes on Day 26 as Iran formally rejected the US 15-point peace plan.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement and Arnault appointment confirmed by UN News, Al Jazeera Day 26, and Pravda EN.

statements.text: I am appointing Jean Arnault as my personal envoy to support a negotiated end to this conflict. The prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz is choking the movement of oil, gas, and fertilizer at a critical moment in the global planting season. This war is out of control. The world cannot afford this to continue.
statements.context: Statement announcing appointment of veteran French diplomat Jean Arnault as UN personal envoy for the Iran-Israel conflict. Arnault previously served as UN envoy to Colombia, Afghanistan, and Myanmar.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by UN News official release. Jean Arnault appointment confirmed.

statements.text: It is high time to end the war — as human suffering deepens, civilian casualties mount, and the global economic impact is increasingly devastating. I have appointed veteran diplomat Jean Arnault as my personal envoy. The world cannot afford a prolonged conflict of this nature.
statements.context: UN Secretary-General statement and announcement of personal envoy appointment
statements.factCheckNote: Guterres statement confirmed via UN Press release. Jean Arnault appointment confirmed.

statements.text: To the United States and to Israel: it's high time to end this war that is risking getting completely out of control. To Iran: stop attacking your neighbors. They were never parties to the conflict. The prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz causes enormous pain to so many people around the world who have nothing to do with this conflict. It is time for the force of the law to prevail over the law of force.
statements.context: Guterres made his most direct appeal to all parties at the EU Council in Brussels, calling on both sides to end the war while simultaneously condemning Iran's attacks on neighboring states.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News and Al Jazeera. Guterres's statements to EU Council are on-the-record.

statements.text: I strongly condemn the killing of UNIFIL peacekeepers. Attacks on peacekeepers are grave violations of international humanitarian law and may amount to war crimes.
statements.context: Response to 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in two separate incidents in southern Lebanon on March 30 — deadliest day for UN forces in the conflict. France called emergency UN Security Council meeting.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News and Al Jazeera.

statements.text: It's time for the force of the law to prevail over the law of force. It's time for diplomacy to prevail over war.
statements.context: Following deaths of 3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers in south Lebanon — called on Iran to stop attacking Gulf neighbors
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News.

statements.text: These incidents are grave violations of international law. This is just one of a number of recent incidents that have jeopardized the safety and security of peacekeepers.
statements.context: After 3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in Lebanon. Called for accountability in phone call with Indonesian FM.
statements.venue: Public statement / phone call with Indonesian FM
statements.factCheckNote: UN News and Euronews confirmed.

statements.text: I am setting up an initiative to allow humanitarian assistance to move through the Strait of Hormuz to prevent a global food crisis.
statements.context: Humanitarian initiative for Strait of Hormuz passage
statements.factCheckNote: AP/KSAT confirmed.

speakers.name: Kaja Kallas
speakers.title: EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs

statements.text: Europe must develop an independent strategic posture on Iran. We cannot simply follow Washington's lead on every crisis.
statements.context: As incoming EU High Representative, Kallas signaled a more autonomous European approach to the Iran file, distinguishing EU policy from the incoming Trump administration.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement accurately reflects the EU's stated intent to maintain strategic autonomy on foreign policy. The EU had already diverged from U.S. Iran policy during the first Trump administration by creating the INSTEX mechanism to preserve trade.

statements.text: The JCPOA was imperfect but it was working. Europe remains committed to a diplomatic solution based on its framework.
statements.context: Kallas reaffirmed European commitment to the diplomatic approach embodied in the JCPOA, contrasting the EU position with the U.S. withdrawal and maximum pressure strategy.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim that the JCPOA 'was working' is partially supported by IAEA data showing Iran was compliant before the U.S. withdrawal. However, the deal had acknowledged limitations including sunset clauses, and Iran had begun exceeding limits even before full U.S. withdrawal took effect.

statements.text: We have opened a direct diplomatic channel with Tehran. Europe will not stand by while others march toward conflict.
statements.context: Kallas announced EU-Iran diplomatic talks, positioning Europe as an active mediator while the U.S. refused direct engagement with Iran.
statements.factCheckDetail: The EU did establish direct diplomatic contacts with Iran through the EEAS. This was confirmed by both European and Iranian diplomatic sources and represented a significant divergence from U.S. policy.

statements.text: Europe will not participate in or support military action against Iran. We believe this path leads only to catastrophe.
statements.context: As military planning accelerated, Kallas explicitly ruled out European participation, creating a significant rift with the U.S. within the NATO alliance.
statements.factCheckDetail: This accurately represents the official EU position as communicated to NATO allies. The statement created documented tensions within the alliance, with the U.S. expressing disappointment at the lack of allied solidarity.

statements.text: Our mediation efforts have produced a framework for talks. All we need is political will from both sides to take the final step.
statements.context: Kallas revealed that European shuttle diplomacy had produced a draft framework for negotiations, but neither Israel nor Iran had committed to participating.
statements.factCheckDetail: The existence of a draft framework was confirmed by European diplomatic sources but its contents were not made public. Claims of being close to a breakthrough were disputed by both Israeli and U.S. officials who described the framework as inadequate.

statements.text: We condemn these strikes in the strongest possible terms. Unilateral military action undermines international law and regional stability.
statements.context: The EU issued one of the sharpest criticisms of Israeli military action, straining EU-Israel relations and drawing criticism from the U.S. for failing to support an ally.
statements.venue: EU Emergency Foreign Affairs Council Statement
statements.factCheckDetail: The condemnation reflects the official EU position. The legal assessment that unilateral strikes may violate international law is supported by many international law scholars, though Israel disputes this characterization based on self-defense provisions.

statements.text: Europe is imposing targeted sanctions on entities involved in the escalation. We are also tripling our humanitarian aid to affected civilian populations.
statements.context: The EU announced a dual-track response combining punitive measures with humanitarian support, positioning Europe as a distinct voice from both the U.S. and China/Russia.
statements.factCheckDetail: The EU did announce a sanctions package and increased humanitarian aid. The specific aid figure and sanctions targets were detailed in official EU Council documents.

statements.text: We are working with the UN Special Envoy on a European peace initiative. A ceasefire must be the first step, not the last.
statements.context: Kallas announced EU coordination with the newly appointed UN Special Envoy, presenting a joint European-UN diplomatic framework as an alternative to the stalled U.S.-led process.
statements.venue: Joint Press Conference with UN Special Envoy, Geneva
statements.factCheckDetail: EU-UN coordination on a peace initiative was confirmed by both organizations. The existence of a diplomatic framework document was verified, though its prospects for acceptance by the warring parties remained uncertain.

statements.text: We propose a Black Sea grain deal-style arrangement for the Strait of Hormuz through the UN. Without action, we face food deprivation next year.
statements.context: Kallas proposed a UN-brokered Hormuz shipping arrangement modeled on the 2022 Black Sea Grain Initiative, warning of catastrophic food security consequences if Hormuz remains disrupted.
statements.venue: EU Foreign Affairs Council / Al Jazeera interview
statements.factCheckNote: Kallas's proposal confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog. The Black Sea grain deal comparison reflects a concrete diplomatic model. Food security warnings align with UN FAO assessments.

statements.text: There is no appetite among European nations for military involvement in the Strait of Hormuz. We propose a Black Sea grain deal model — a humanitarian oil corridor under UN auspices.
statements.context: EU High Representative rejected military involvement in Hormuz, proposing instead a diplomatic framework modeled on the Black Sea grain deal. EU foreign ministers voted against expanding Aspides naval mission.
statements.factCheckNote: EU rejection of Hormuz military role confirmed. Germany's Pistorius explicitly stated 'This is not our war.'

statements.text: We have been consulting with Gulf countries, Jordan, Egypt about whether we could bring forward proposals for Iran, Israel and the US to get out of this situation so that everybody saves face.
statements.context: Kallas called on US and Israel to end the war in a Reuters exclusive. Said EU is consulting regional governments on a face-saving diplomatic exit. Also discussed with UN Secretary-General Guterres a Black Sea grain deal-style arrangement for Hormuz oil transit.
statements.factCheckNote: Reuters exclusive confirmed by Fox News and Al-Monitor. Kallas confirmed consulting with Gulf states on diplomatic off-ramp.

statements.text: There is no appetite in Europe to expand our naval presence or join this military campaign. Europe will not allow itself to be blackmailed.
statements.context: EU summit in Brussels; EU refuses to join US military campaign or secure Hormuz. Kallas refers to Austrian Chancellor Stocker's wording.
statements.factCheckNote: WaPo confirmed

statements.text: Iran bears a heavy responsibility for the current escalation.
statements.context: Statement at UNSC during Resolution 2817 debate. France voted in favor of condemning Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Official statement at UN, consistent with EU position

statements.text: Member states do not have an appetite to go to this war.
statements.context: After EU Foreign Ministers decided against expanding Aspides naval mission to Hormuz
statements.factCheckNote: Multiple EU press pool reports

statements.text: EU 'no appetite' to join military campaign. European Council: moratorium on energy/water strikes, reopen Hormuz. ECB: 'material impact on near-term inflation.'
statements.context: European Council summit
statements.factCheckNote: Council conclusions published

statements.text: Iran is responsible for decades of violence and the fewer options Iran has to terrorise the region, the better. The regime is weaker than it has been for many years, but there is no clear trajectory how this war will end. The EU unanimously calls for maximum restraint and de-escalation, and supports reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Statement at EU Brussels summit where all 27 EU heads of state called for a moratorium on energy/water facility strikes and reopening of Strait of Hormuz. EU also approved new sanctions against 19 Iranian officials.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Sofia Globe, US News. EU Council conclusions published.

statements.text: The EU continues to hold Iran accountable. Today, EU Member States ambassadors approved new sanctions targeting 19 regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. We stand firm in calling for an immediate ceasefire and opening of the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Posted on X on Day 23. Kallas also held calls with Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea on Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: EU sanctions vote confirmed. Kallas diplomatic calls confirmed by EEAS.

statements.text: The EU is engaged in intensive diplomacy to address the crisis. I held calls today with Foreign Minister Araghchi, and with counterparts in Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea, covering the conflict, energy infrastructure strikes, and the Hormuz pressure.
statements.context: Kallas provided summary of her diplomatic outreach on March 22, as Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran over Hormuz dominated international attention.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ANI News reporting on Spanish PM Sanchez and EU diplomatic activity March 22-23.

statements.text: Today, EU Member States ambassadors approved new sanctions targeting 19 regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. Iran is an exporter of war. This is not Europe's war, but Europe's interests are directly at stake.
statements.context: EU approved fresh sanctions against 19 Iranian officials and entities. Kallas also announced Rubio will attend G7 foreign ministers meeting in France this week. EU has now sanctioned 263 Iranian individuals and 53 entities.
statements.factCheckNote: EU sanctions confirmed by Kallas X post and EEAS press release.

statements.text: Expanding the Aspides naval mission is the fastest way to boost Hormuz security, but nobody is ready to put their people in harm's way. This war was started without consulting the EU and despite the bloc's calls for restraint. Europe supports diplomacy but cannot be asked to endorse an armed escalation that we had no voice in starting.
statements.context: Statement before EU foreign ministers meeting in Brussels. Kallas acknowledged no EU member states are willing to deploy forces to the Strait of Hormuz despite pressure from Asian oil-importing nations.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by NBC News liveblog from Brussels meeting.
statements.source: NBC News — Live updates: Iran war talks

statements.text: This is not Europe's war. We didn't start the war. There is no appetite among European leaders to expand our naval force to Hormuz or join the fighting.
statements.context: G7 foreign ministers meeting at Abbaye des Vaux-de-Cernay, France. Kallas joined the G7 to address the Iran war alongside Germany's Pistorius who called it 'a catastrophe for the world's economies.' Secretary Rubio attended to 'sell' the war to skeptical allies.
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers Meeting, Versailles, France
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via CNBC G7 reporting, Modern Diplomacy. Kallas's 'not Europe's war' framing is consistent with EU's position throughout the conflict.

statements.text: We see that Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans, and Russia is also supporting Iran now with the drones.
statements.context: Statement at the G7 foreign ministers meeting in Vaux-de-Cernay, France. Kallas linked the Iran and Ukraine wars directly: "If America wants the war in the Middle East to stop, Iran to stop attacking them, they should also put the pressure on Russia." UK Defense Secretary John Healey confirmed the claim independently, citing British intelligence. US special envoy Steve Witkoff diverged sharply, saying Russia denied it and the US would take them "at their word."
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News, Bloomberg, Euronews confirmed the Kallas statement. UK confirmation from Healey cited by Bloomberg. US-EU divergence on the Russia angle is itself notable.

statements.text: We see that Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans, and Russia is also supporting Iran now with the drones so that they can attack neighbouring countries and also US military bases.
statements.context: Statement at G7 foreign ministers meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France. UK Defense Secretary John Healey and France FM Barrot separately confirmed the claim.
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers Meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed independently by UK (Healey: intelligence-backed) and France (Barrot: credible evidence). Russia denied. US envoy Witkoff took Russia's denial at face value, creating stark US-EU intelligence divergence.
statements.source: Bloomberg — Russia Helps Iran Kill Americans, EU Top Diplomat Kallas Says

statements.text: If America wants the war in the Middle East to stop, Iran to stop attacking them, they should also put the pressure on Russia so that they are not able to help them in this. Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans.
statements.context: Statement at G7 foreign ministers meeting in France, calling on the US to pressure Russia for providing Iran intelligence used to target American forces. G7 issued joint statement calling for Hormuz reopening and civilian protection.
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers Meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France
statements.factCheckNote: Kallas confirmed by Times of Israel, France 24. UK intelligence confirmed Russian assistance per Bloomberg. Rubio downplayed the impact saying Russia denied it.

statements.text: There was no appetite among G7 members for extending the Aspides mission or entering this war actively. Nobody wants to go actively in this war. The G7 agreed we should secure the Strait of Hormuz — but only after the war ends. We are working with regional partners to find a diplomatic path.
statements.context: Post-G7 Foreign Ministers meeting press conference in France

statements.text: The EU continues to hold Iran accountable.
